name: Plaça d'Espanya / lower Montjuic approach
access: Walk + metro/bus possible; simplest is walk/metro early, then stay put.
bikeUsefulness: Bike not very useful; road closures and crowds make locking/returning annoying.
viewingQuality: Very good: decisive late TTT approach before Montjuic, easier than the finish.
crowding: High but more manageable than Olympic finish/Sagrada Família.
amenities: Good urban backup: Plaça d'Espanya / Arenas / Montjuic facilities nearby; shade variable.
roadClosureRisk: Moderate-high: major junction and final approach roads will close; arrive before caravan.
exitStrategy: Walk back or use metro after crowd disperses; avoid trying to cross the course.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Best balance from prior planning: close enough to lodging, meaningful race action, not the absolute finish scrum.

name: Upper Montjuic / Olympic Stadium final climb area
access: Walk/metro to Plaça d'Espanya area then walk uphill; plan extra time.
bikeUsefulness: Not useful unless arriving very early and accepting complicated post-race exit.
viewingQuality: Excellent: final climb/finish energy; riders near full effort.
crowding: Very high; finish-zone pinch points likely.
amenities: Stadium/park facilities may exist but access-controlled; food/water may require pre-planning; shade patchy.
roadClosureRisk: High: finish-zone closures and spectator controls likely.
exitStrategy: Slow walk downhill after last teams; expect bottlenecks toward Plaça d'Espanya.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Great if race atmosphere is the goal; less comfortable for a mixed group or if heat/fatigue matters.

name: Sagrada Familia mid-course / time-check area
access: Walkable from hotel; very easy transit fallback.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary; crowds and security perimeter make it more hassle than help.
publicityCaravan: Yes - on official route / time check
viewingQuality: Good TTT viewing: mid-course speed and official time-check context; less decisive than Montjuic.
crowding: Very high because it combines iconic tourist area + race route.
amenities: Many cafés/shops nearby, but bathrooms and water lines likely stressed; shade varies by block.
roadClosureRisk: Moderate-high: easy to reach on foot, but crossing restrictions around the basilica likely.
exitStrategy: Walk back north/east away from route; avoid trying to cross immediately.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Good if Craig/Thy or family want the most Barcelona-feeling backdrop; not the lowest-stress option.

name: Seafront / Port Olimpic early-route area
access: Walk/taxi-transit depending closures; flatter than Montjuic.
bikeUsefulness: Bike only mildly useful if arriving very early; locking/closures still a nuisance.
viewingQuality: Good speed spectacle and easier space, but less tactically decisive than final climb.
crowding: Moderate-high; likely less compressed than finish/Sagrada Família.
amenities: Beachfront restaurants/kiosks; bathrooms around beach/marina but capacity uncertain; sun exposure high.
roadClosureRisk: Moderate: early-route roads close but more escape directions than Montjuic.
exitStrategy: Exit along seafront or back toward El Born; avoid course crossings.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Good fallback if the group wants lower stress, water views, and easier movement.

name: Cote du Chateau de Montjuic / Montjuic Castle climb zone
access: Metro/walk to Montjuic, then walk uphill; exact roadside access needs verification.
bikeUsefulness: Bike possible early, but likely annoying once closures/crowds build; walking is safer.
viewingQuality: Excellent: repeated 1.6 km climb at 9.3%; likely attacks and split-making.
crowding: Very high, especially near KOM points and road bends.
amenities: Park has some facilities but access may be controlled; bring water/snacks; shade better than open boulevards.
roadClosureRisk: High: Montjuic loop, climb, and finish controls; exact spectator corridors TBD.
exitStrategy: Walk downhill after race; avoid relying on bikes or taxis from the hill.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: This preserves the original Montjuic Castle itinerary idea; watch official spectator access when Barcelona releases local plans.

name: Olympic Stadium finish / final line area
access: Walk/metro to Montjuic; arrive very early if trying for barriers.
bikeUsefulness: Bike is a downside unless parked far away before closures.
publicityCaravan: Yes - multiple official passes and finish
viewingQuality: Excellent for finish atmosphere and potentially multiple line passages.
crowding: Extreme; expect controlled zones and bottlenecks.
amenities: Facilities may be present but queues likely; bring water; shade variable.
roadClosureRisk: Very high: finish-zone security and post-race crowd release.
exitStrategy: Slow downhill walk; stay patient and avoid tight transit points immediately after finish.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Best if the group specifically wants the finish; otherwise climb/lower circuit may be more practical.

name: Fontaines de Montjuic / lower circuit entry
access: Metro/walk to Plaça d'Espanya; easiest Montjuic-adjacent option.
bikeUsefulness: Bike not needed; parking/lockup likely not worth it.
viewingQuality: Very good: circuit entry, wider area, likely multiple sightings; less pure climbing drama.
crowding: High but more navigable than castle/finish.
amenities: Good nearby urban amenities, Magic Fountain/Plaça d'Espanya area; shade mixed.
roadClosureRisk: Moderate-high: easiest to reach, but course crossings limited.
exitStrategy: Walk/metro from Plaça d'Espanya after crowds thin.
luggage: Hotel base already in Barcelona; no luggage issue.
notes: Likely the most balanced Stage 2 option if access to the upper climb is too restricted.

name: Puigcerda town / N-260 near N-152/N-154
access: Walk from Hotel Villa Paulita; no driving needed once parked/checked in.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary; walking from lodging is the win.
viewingQuality: Good practical viewing: town energy, official route, easy logistics; less mountain drama than Col de Toses.
crowding: Moderate-high but manageable for a small town; arrive early.
amenities: Best amenities: cafés, shops, hotel access, bathrooms; shade depends block.
roadClosureRisk: Low-medium if already in town; high if trying to drive in late.
exitStrategy: Walk back to hotel after race; minimal exit friction.
luggage: Strongest ability to drop luggage/check in or leave bags at hotel before spectating.
notes: Original itinerary says drive very early and spectate in Puigcerda; this option preserves that low-friction plan.

name: La Molina / Col de Toses area
distanceFromBase: ~10-18 mi by road from Hotel Villa Paulita, depending exact roadside / pass
access: Drive/bike only if leaving very early; road closures make late movement risky.
bikeUsefulness: Bike can be useful for committed riders: ride from Puigcerda/Alp area before closure, but it is a real mountain day.
viewingQuality: Excellent: Category 1 Col de Toses, climbing speed, mountain scenery.
crowding: High at the summit/pass; potentially more spread out on lower slopes.
amenities: Sparse; bring all food/water/layers; bathrooms limited to villages/station facilities.
roadClosureRisk: High: mountain-road closure and parking constraints.
exitStrategy: Bike/walk out after course reopens; driving exit likely slow.
luggage: Check-in/luggage more difficult; coordinate with hotel before leaving town.
notes: Only choose this if the group is willing to trade comfort for a real climb-viewing experience.

name: Ur / Angoustrine / Targasonne after Puigcerda
distanceFromBase: ~6-12 mi by road from Hotel Villa Paulita, depending exact village
access: Drive/bike from Puigcerda only if crossing/road access is confirmed early; otherwise risky.
bikeUsefulness: Bike can help if roads close to cars, but cross-border logistics and return timing matter.
viewingQuality: Very good: later-stage tension, open views, less town congestion than Puigcerda.
crowding: Moderate; likely lower than finish/major climbs.
amenities: Village-level amenities only; bring water/snacks; shade variable.
roadClosureRisk: Medium-high: cross-border road closures can trap you.
exitStrategy: Return to Puigcerda may be delayed until route clears; have patience.
luggage: Check-in first if possible; do not rely on driving back quickly.
notes: Good if Puigcerda feels too tame and you can confirm the road plan.

name: Font-Romeu / Col du Calvaire
distanceFromBase: ~14-18 mi by road from Hotel Villa Paulita
access: Longer transfer from Puigcerda; only with early departure and closure confirmation.
bikeUsefulness: Bike is possible for strong riders but makes it a bigger day; car access may be limited.
viewingQuality: Very good: categorized Col du Calvaire late in stage, more race selection than Puigcerda.
crowding: High at KOM/obvious corners, manageable in town.
amenities: Better than remote pass: town amenities nearby; bring water anyway.
roadClosureRisk: High: later mountain roads and French-side closures.
exitStrategy: Delayed return likely; consider dinner/check-in timing.
luggage: Harder to use Hotel Villa Paulita as a rest base.
notes: Added because it is on the official timetable and gives a late categorized climb without going all the way to Les Angles.

name: Les Angles finish / Pla del Mir
distanceFromBase: ~20-25 mi by road from Hotel Villa Paulita
access: Drive very early only; expect finish-zone access controls.
bikeUsefulness: Bike only for a committed long mountain day; not family-friendly logistics.
viewingQuality: Excellent finish drama and final 1.8 km at 6.5%.
crowding: Very high at finish, plus limited mountain-town capacity.
amenities: Finish-town amenities, but queues and controlled zones likely; bring supplies.
roadClosureRisk: Very high: finish road closures and post-stage traffic.
exitStrategy: Expect a slow exit after the finish; avoid if needing an easy evening in Puigcerda.
luggage: Worst option for easy check-in/return to hotel.
notes: Likely not preferred unless you prioritize the finish above all logistics.

name: Arreau / D918 valley fallback
distanceFromBase: ~0.0 mi from Arreau town center / Airbnb area
access: Walk from Arreau base if your Airbnb is near town; otherwise short bike/walk.
bikeUsefulness: Bike useful only for local positioning; not necessary to see the race.
viewingQuality: Good practical viewing, but before the Aspin/Tourmalet fireworks.
crowding: Moderate; town will be busy but less intense than the climbs.
amenities: Best amenities: town cafés, bathrooms, water, ability to retreat; shade variable.
roadClosureRisk: Low if staying in town; avoid moving cars on race route.
exitStrategy: Walk back to Airbnb; easiest exit of the day.
luggage: Excellent; can stay based in Arreau.
notes: If mother/family comfort matters, this is the control option. Saint-Lary itself is not on the listed route; Campan/Sainte-Marie-de-Campan are on-route alternatives after Aspin.

name: Col d'Aspin area from Arreau
distanceFromBase: ~7-8 mi by road from Arreau town center to the summit area
access: Bike from Arreau before closure; walking not realistic unless staying nearby.
bikeUsefulness: Useful: a clear bike-to-climb plan from Arreau, less extreme than Tourmalet.
viewingQuality: Excellent: Category 1 climb, first big mountain selection, close to lodging.
crowding: High on summit; lower slopes may be more manageable.
amenities: Sparse on climb; bring all food/water/layers; limited shade at open areas.
roadClosureRisk: High for cars; manageable by bike if early and allowed before closure.
exitStrategy: Descend/return to Arreau only when permitted; expect delays and crowded descent.
luggage: Good if already settled at Airbnb; no check-in issue.
notes: Likely the best real mountain stage choice from Arreau without the full Tourmalet commitment.

name: Tourmalet summit / La Mongie side
distanceFromBase: ~30 mi one-way by bike via Aspin/Campan/La Mongie; huge climbing day
access: Bike very early from Arreau via Aspin/Campan/La Mongie if fit and road rules allow; car not practical.
bikeUsefulness: Useful but demanding: long ride with major climbing; plan lights/layers/food and a conservative turnaround.
viewingQuality: Elite: HC Tourmalet/Souvenir Jacques Goddet; iconic day.
crowding: Very high, especially summit and La Mongie.
amenities: Sparse/overloaded: some La Mongie facilities, but queues; summit exposed; bring everything.
roadClosureRisk: Very high: mountain roads may close early; descending after race can be chaotic.
exitStrategy: Return by bike after roads open; expect slow crowded descent and possible darkness contingency.
luggage: Only if fully settled and prepared the night before.
notes: The itinerary asks whether this is possible. Answer: possible only as an all-day, very early, strong-rider plan; not a casual outing.

name: Bareges / Luz-Saint-Sauveur side after Tourmalet descent
priority: Good race action / poor base logistics
distanceFromBase: ~35-45+ mi by road/bike from Arreau via mountain passes; not a simple out-and-back
access: Not practical from Arreau unless repositioning very early by car/bike before closures.
bikeUsefulness: Bike makes it an enormous day; car likely trapped by closures.
viewingQuality: Very good: post-Tourmalet descent/transition, but not the summit itself.
crowding: High in towns and obvious bends.
amenities: Town amenities in Bareges/Luz, but access/queues likely; shade variable.
roadClosureRisk: Very high because you are on the far side of Tourmalet from lodging.
exitStrategy: Long delayed exit; returning to Arreau after the stage is awkward.
luggage: Poor fit for Arreau lodging.
notes: Useful only if closure plans make Tourmalet impossible and you choose to relocate early.

name: Gavarnie-Gedre finish
distanceFromBase: ~50+ mi by road from Arreau depending route; not practical once closures begin
access: Drive/reposition extremely early; not practical as a same-day move from Arreau during closures.
bikeUsefulness: Bike from Arreau is not realistic for most groups on race day.
viewingQuality: Excellent finish setting near Cirque de Gavarnie; final climb stakes.
crowding: Very high with limited valley capacity.
amenities: Finish-town amenities limited and overloaded; bring supplies/layers.
roadClosureRisk: Extreme: finish-zone and valley-road closure risk.
exitStrategy: Slowest exit; likely late return.
luggage: Poor fit unless staying closer to Gavarnie.
notes: Spectacular on paper, but likely not worth the friction from Arreau compared with Aspin/Tourmalet.

name: Finish / Place des Quinconces area
access: Walk/tram from hotel if service runs; walking may be simplest due to closures.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary; mother joins, and final-crowd logistics make bikes a hassle.
viewingQuality: Best for sprint finish and podium atmosphere.
crowding: Very high; arrive early for barriers.
amenities: City-center amenities but huge queues; bring water; shade limited along open spaces.
roadClosureRisk: High: finish and quays will have security/traffic controls.
exitStrategy: Walk back west/north after crowd dispersal; avoid trying to cross route immediately.
luggage: Potentially can drop luggage/check in first if arrival from Pyrenees is early enough; otherwise hotel should hold bags.
notes: Worth doing if the group still has energy after the long drive; make this the high-reward option.

name: Pont Simone Veil / approach into Bordeaux
access: Transit/walk/taxi as close as allowed, then walk; less convenient than center from Burdigala.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary, especially with mother joining.
viewingQuality: Very good: bridge crossing and final 4.5 km approach, likely lead-out tension.
crowding: High but probably less dense than the finish barriers.
amenities: Limited on the bridge; use nearby right/left-bank amenities before staking out; exposed sun/wind.
roadClosureRisk: Medium-high: bridge access may be tightly controlled.
exitStrategy: Exit by walking away from route toward tram/bus options after roads reopen.
luggage: Drop luggage first only if schedule allows; otherwise hotel bag hold.
notes: Bordeaux Tourism highlights the new Simone-Veil bridge as part of the city approach.

name: Riverside final 3-5 km approach / quays
access: Walk/transit from Burdigala; choose exact block after local route map/closures publish.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary; could be more trouble than walking.
publicityCaravan: Yes - if positioned on official final approach
viewingQuality: Very good: high-speed lead-out, Garonne backdrop, easier than finish line.
crowding: High but spread along quays; likely more choice than the final 500 m.
amenities: Good urban amenities nearby, but queues; shade limited in open quay sections.
roadClosureRisk: Medium-high: route-side closures but many walking escape routes.
exitStrategy: Walk back through side streets once crowds thin; easier than finish-line crush.
luggage: Best if tired after drive but still want live race rather than TV.
notes: Strong balanced choice if the finish area is too crowded or the group arrives late.

name: Bar / fan-zone viewing if tired after drive
priority: Lowest stress / may miss live caravan
access: Walk from hotel to a bar/fan-zone; prioritize comfort and bathrooms.
bikeUsefulness: Bike unnecessary.
publicityCaravan: Depends: no if inside watching TV; yes only if the bar/fan-zone is directly on the route/quays.
caravanTime: If on-route, likely ≈15:45-15:52; otherwise no live caravan.
raceTime: TV/live ambience; race passes only if route-side.
viewingQuality: Comfort-first; best if drive fatigue outweighs need for barriers.
crowding: Variable; fan zones can be crowded, but indoor/bar option is easier.
amenities: Best amenities: bathrooms, food, water, shade/AC depending venue.
roadClosureRisk: Low if not trying to cross the course; medium if route-adjacent.
exitStrategy: Walk back to hotel whenever ready.
luggage: Best for luggage/check-in uncertainty after the long drive.
notes: Useful fallback. Your itinerary already notes downtown wine/bar interest in Bordeaux.
